behaviors.description: The payload sent into summary generation contains metrics, issue tracker, and deployment-log data together.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure is at a module handoff boundary (collector aggregation -> summary input), not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network needed if each source client is stubbed in-process. MOCK-HIDE: unit-testing only the merge helper could miss wiring omissions between modules. LLM-DEP rejected: behavior text is about data passed to the step, not model output quality. MULTI-STEP rejected: single orchestration step, not multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real internal modules for the weekly job with three in-process source fakes returning distinct sentinel fields; assert the exact summary-input object passed downstream includes all three source sections and preserves source identity.

behaviors.description: If one source is unavailable, report generation continues and marks that section as `data unavailable` instead of aborting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when fetch, error handling, and report composer interact. REAL-INFRA rejected: source unavailability can be reproduced with an in-process failing client; no real network outage required to catch fallback logic bugs. MOCK-HIDE: a unit test of only formatter/error helper could miss pipeline-abort regressions. LLM-DEP rejected by behavior text: requirement is control-flow fallback and section note, not model judgment quality. MULTI-STEP rejected: no multi-agentic chain required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the assembled reporting pipeline with one source client raising timeout/503 and two clients succeeding; assert process exits successfully, final report contains `data unavailable` for the failed section, and other sections still contain fresh data.

behaviors.description: Narrative summary accurately reflects key numeric/status values in the provided input data.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on what the real summary model generates (possible omissions/misstatements vary run-to-run). MOCK-HIDE: deterministic stubs always pass and cannot reveal hallucination or value drift. REAL-INFRA is secondary; even if transport is stable, model-output validity is the failure boundary. MULTI-STEP rejected: behavior concerns one generation decision, not a chained agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Using fixed representative weekly datasets with known key facts, call the real summary-generation model and score outputs against an extraction rubric (presence/correctness of key values, no contradictory claims) across repeated runs; fail if accuracy/consistency threshold is breached.

behaviors.description: End-to-end run (collection -> summary -> email) delivers a complete report to configured recipients' inbox.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: failure boundary includes real email transport/inbox behavior and external API interactions. MOCK-HIDE: mocked mail sender can pass while real SMTP/API auth, formatting, or deliverability fails. LLM-DEP rejected by behavior text: this checks delivery completion to inbox, not quality of model decisions. MULTI-STEP rejected: not validating sequential agentic decision-making.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled script in a production-like environment against reachable test endpoints and a real test mailbox; verify message arrival in inbox for configured recipients and validate subject/body include all expected report sections.

behaviors.description: If email service is unreachable, persist generated report locally and retry delivery on next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: requires filesystem persistence across runs plus real unreachable-delivery behavior and retry on later schedule/process invocation. MOCK-HIDE: in-memory fakes can miss path/permission/atomic-write and cross-run state bugs. LLM-DEP rejected: requirement is delivery-failure recovery mechanics, not model output correctness. MULTI-STEP rejected: retry workflow is temporal/system orchestration, not multiple agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run once with mail endpoint intentionally unreachable; assert report file is written to real local path. Run next scheduled execution with mail restored; assert previously saved report is retried and successfully delivered, then retry artifact/state is cleared or marked sent.
